sections.heading: Education
items: Ph.D. in Communication, University of Westminster, UK
M.A. in Journalism, National Chengchi University (NCCU), Taiwan

sections.heading: Research Areas
sections.content: Prof. Lin's scholarship centers on the political economy of media, with particular attention to Taiwan's media industry history, public service broadcasting, and the dynamics of press freedom in East Asian contexts. Her work applies critical discourse analysis and qualitative research methodologies to examine media regulation, television systems, and the relationship between media capital and democratic governance.

sections.heading: Professional Experience
items: Director, Graduate Institute of Journalism, NTU (2009–2012)
Assistant Professor, Fu Jen Catholic University (1998–2005)

sections.heading: Selected Publications
items: Over 25 peer-reviewed journal articles on media regulation, television systems, and press freedom in Taiwan and comparative contexts
Research on television news channel regulation and media capital in Taiwan
Numerous book chapters in edited volumes on communication policy and public service media

sections.heading: Honors & Awards
items: Distinguished Teaching Award, National Taiwan University (2017)
